description:
You are a Computer Systems Analyst for a state court administration office. The COURT_ADMIN schema (connected as court_admin/Court2024 to XEPDB1) contains a legacy case management database. Before migrating to a new system, you must audit the data, fix all integrity issues, and implement preventive controls.

Audit and resolve the following data quality issues within the database:
- Orphaned Records: Delete hearings referencing non-existent case_id values and set case_parties referencing non-existent attorney_id values to NULL.
- Temporal Violations: Ensure hearings are not scheduled before their case's filing_date and that disposition_date is not earlier than filing_date.
- Status Inconsistencies: Cancel future-dated hearings for 'CLOSED' cases and update cases with a recorded disposition and disposition_date to 'CLOSED' status.
- Duplicates: Identify cases where the same lead party (PLAINTIFF) filed the same case_type within 30 days and mark the newer cases with status 'DUPLICATE'.
- Fee Anomalies: Fix overpayments by setting paid_amount equal to amount and change fee status to 'WAIVED' for 'UNPAID' fees on cases closed more than 90 days ago.

Log all discovered issues in a table named DATA_QUALITY_ISSUES (issue_id NUMBER PK, issue_category VARCHAR2, table_name VARCHAR2, record_id NUMBER, description VARCHAR2, severity VARCHAR2, detected_date DATE, resolved VARCHAR2). Apply DML fixes to the source tables and create a view named CASE_INTEGRITY_VW that validates these rules in real-time (returning zero rows when fixes are complete). Implement at least two preventive triggers to maintain these integrity rules. Export a summary report containing issue categories and counts to /home/ga/court_audit_report.csv.
